subject: fuse/io-uring: Proposal to support pBuf in additon to kBuf

reviews.author: Bernd Schubert
reviews.summary: Reviewer Bernd Schubert noted that he thought Jens had already accepted the kBuf changes and was unaware of the change from v1 to v2, which dropped the use of kBuf in favor of user-allocated buffers.
reviews.raw_body:
yes absolutely it does. I had actually thought that Jens had already
accepted the kBuf changes. I had seen the discussion with Pavel, but
then seen (at least I think) that Jens had accepted it. And with the
different versions, I didn't notice that v2 (in my counting that is v5,
I think), doesn't use kBuf anymore.

If I understand it right, the io-uring bvec changes are only needed for
zero-copy?

Thanks,
Bernd

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary:
Author acknowledged that the patch was previously merged but later dropped, providing no further explanation or plan for reintegration.

Author confirms that the issue of mlock requirement has been addressed in v2 by dropping kernel-managed buffers as an io-uring native infrastructure and instead allowing user allocations during registration time.
reviews.raw_body:
Yes it had been briefly merged into Jens's io_uring/for-7.1 staging
tree and then it was dropped.

---

Yes, that is correct.

Thanks,
Joanne

2026-04-14.patch_summary: This patch proposes to support pBuf in addition to kBuf for the fuse and io-uring interfaces. The change comes from a revised approach where kernel-managed buffers are no longer an integral part of the io-uring infrastructure, but rather self-contained within the fuse implementation. This design allows for user-allocated buffers to be passed during registration time, with optional pinning for optimization. Zero-copy requires both header and payload buffer pinning, which is subject to CAP_SYS_ADMIN privileges. The patch addresses concerns about mlock requirements and aims to maintain compatibility with existing fuse and io-uring contracts.
